prompt: Analyze current NFL accounting for signing bonuses, option bonuses, and void years. Provide 5 fully worked contract examples with year-by-year cap hits and dead money, citing authoritative sources.
Explain June 1 designation mechanics vs. in-season release/trade. Include timeline rules, cash vs. cap, and two numeric scenarios with tables.
Map all contract components (base, signing, option, roster, workout, LTBE/NTLBE incentives) to cap treatment. Provide a validator checklist we can turn into unit tests.
Detail restructure tools (simple conversion, option bonuses, void-year adds) with constraints. Provide 3 before/after cap tables including prorations and future dead money.
Summarize rookie contract rules (round/slot), fifth-year option, and UFA/RFA tenders. Include worked examples and edge cases.
Compare Spotrac vs. OverTheCap methodologies for cap figures; list discrepancies and how to reconcile them programmatically.
Design JSON schemas for contract objects that capture all cap-relevant terms. Include field types, constraints, and example payloads.
Research void-year usage trends (2019–present). Quantify frequency, average lengths, and risks; provide references.
Explain NLTBE vs. LTBE incentives with examples; show how postseason awards/escalators flow into next-year cap.
Document trade mechanics: pre/post-June 1, guaranteed salary offsets, injury guarantees. Provide tables.
Define algorithm to compute dead money for any transaction type. Provide pseudocode and test vectors.
Summarize CBA rules on guaranteed salary (skill/cap/injury). Include examples of partial guarantees and vesting dates.
Outline approach to simulate multi-year cap strategy with rolling window, including expected cap growth assumptions and risk metrics.
Explain practice squad/elevations and their cap impact. Provide formulae and a reference table.
Research post-season share payments and their interaction (if any) with team cap; clarify misconceptions with citations.
Detail roster cut-down economics (90→53) and the financial implications of IR, PUP, NFI; include cap accounting pathways.
Compare franchise/transition tag formulas and historical usage since 2011 CBA; include position outliers.
Create a compliance checklist for cap/legal validation before approving any simulated transaction.
Survey empirical predictors of NFL success by position (QB, OT, EDGE, WR). Include at least 3 rigorous sources per position and note disagreements.
Design a fog-of-war model for scouting where reports narrow intervals with investment. Provide math and JSON fields we can store.
Map combine/pro-day drills to position-relevant traits. Provide weighting ranges and caveats with sources.
Define bust/hidden-gem distributions and how to sample them by position/round. Include calibration guidance.
Summarize interview/psychometrics best practices and how to reflect them in-game as hidden traits and reveal triggers.
Draft board mechanics: BPA vs. need, future value discounting, and trade-up/down logic with acceptance tests.
Research how teams quantify scheme fit (zone vs. man CB, gap vs. zone OL). Provide mapping to performance multipliers.
Study injury history as predictor; propose position-specific risk curves and mitigation strategies (medical grades).
Build a cross-check protocol (film vs. data vs. interviews). Provide a scoring rubric and reviewer roles.
Summarize historical draft capital value charts (JJ, Fitzgerald-Spielberger, PFF). Provide conversion code sketch.
Design UDFA discovery and bidding. Include scouting noise and fit bonuses.
Outline a realistic mock week of a GM pre-draft (meetings, board updates, medical, trade prep).
Create a schema for a Prospect object including hidden and revealed fields, audit trail of info sources.
Define acceptance tests for draft outcomes quality (variance, hit rates by round) compared to real NFL baselines.
Specify Analytics Architect decision policy (objective, constraints, risk). Output JSON + rationale with citations.
Specify Culture Commander policy including locker-room signals and penalties for 'bad apple' risk.
Specify Strategic Rebuilder long-horizon asset model, discounting, and tolerance bands.
Specify Player-Centric Collaborator negotiation heuristics and escalation paths with agents.
Specify Aggressive Dealmaker trade-up/trade-for-star triggers and stop-loss conditions.
Design hybrid/archetype blending logic with context switches (window, injuries, coaching changes).
Define a learning loop for AI GMs that adjusts weights from outcomes; include safe-guards to avoid exploitation loops.
Create evaluation metrics/KPIs for AI GMs across 10-season sims; include fairness checks.
Draft human-readable 'personas' that map to machine policies; include examples of signature moves.
Outline an agent-vs-agent trade negotiation protocol with reservation prices and time pressure.
Define simulation hooks for ownership pressure and media that influence GM decisions ethically.
Propose test scenarios to validate each archetype behaves distinctively and logically.
Survey trade value modeling in the NFL (picks vs. players). Provide a composite approach and calibration plan.
Design multi-asset trade evaluation (picks, players, cash-like cap effects). Include veto guards.
Build a fair-trade detector for CPU proposals with configurable tolerance and context.
Define player surplus value estimation using contract terms and aging curves by position.
Model 'contender premium' and 'deadline pressure' on trade prices; provide equations.
Propose a conflict-resolution mechanism when two CPU teams bid for the same player.
Create acceptance tests: no trades violate cap/roster constraints; sanity bounds on value delta.
Design pick-for-pick trade-down ladders consistent with historical data.
Quantify uncertainty in player evaluation and propagate it into trade valuations.
Document rare-but-real trade structures and how to represent them (conditional picks, de-escalators).
Define 53-man rules, practice squad, elevations, gameday actives. Provide validation logic ready for unit tests.
Design depth-chart constraints per position group; include emergency QB rules and OL versatility mapping.
Create progression/regression curves by position and age; include uncertainty bands.
Map scheme fit (offense/defense) to role-based multipliers; include penalties for misfit usage.
Outline preseason cut and waiver claim simulation with realistic claim priority and cap checks.
Define position flex 'badges' and how they interact with injuries and depth chart logic.
Model leadership/locker room impact as team-level modifiers; ensure realism and guardrails.
Acceptance tests: season runs without invalid rosters; violations emit actionable errors.
Survey injury incidence by position and body area; create baseline probabilities and recovery times.
Model recurring/compounding injuries and practice limitations; provide state machine diagrams.
Design questionable/doubtful/out tags and elevation decisions; calibrate to realistic rates.
Quantify performance penalties while playing hurt; include tapering curves.
Integrate training staff quality as a modifier on injuries and time-to-return.
Acceptance tests: injury simulation reproduces historical season totals within tolerance.
Define determinism contract: seed in/seed out, fingerprinting, and CI checks across N seeds.
Design a team-strength model (off/def/ST) that produces realistic score distributions by opponent strength.
Propose overtime and no-tie logic consistent with current NFL rules; include acceptance tests.
Create a season schedule generator honoring divisional play, byes, and constraints; verify consistency.
Define playoff seeding & tiebreakers; provide edge cases and test vectors.
Model weather/home-field impacts at a macro level without play-by-play; cite studies for effect sizes.
Acceptance tests: season sim speed (<1s), determinism across runs, distribution checks.
Telemetry plan: capture summary stats and anomalies for balancing.
Propose the Universal Simulation Engine module boundaries and data contracts (Players, Contracts, Cap, Draft, Trade, Sim).
Define persistence format (JSON/SQLite/Postgres) and migration strategy for save files.
Specify event bus and subsystem integration points; include sequence diagrams.
Design a property-based testing approach for engine invariants (cap compliance, roster validity).
Plan seed management for reproducibility across subsystems and versions.
Security & integrity: checksums, anti-tamper for competitive leagues, and audit logs.
Clarify DMCA safe harbor for UGC (logos, names). Provide checklist and draft policy text.
Survey public/licensable data sources for player/team info; note license terms and API limits.
Define privacy & data retention policies aligned with US/EU norms for user data and telemetry.
Compose terms clarifying AI-generated content ownership and user responsibilities.
Draft GM Dashboard IA: what cards, KPIs, and actions belong on the landing screen; reference comparable games.
Design Cap/Contracts UI with drill-down from team cap table → individual contract → restructure flow.
Create Draft Board UX with fog-of-war visuals, interviews, and visit tracking.
Outline Trade Center UX with fairness meter, cap compliance checker, and CPU offer rationale.
Accessibility checklist for color/contrast, keyboard nav, and motion reduction.
Design a News/Events feed that avoids spam and conveys meaningful signals.
Select stack (FastAPI/Node) and design the API for the MVP features; include OpenAPI skeleton.
Choose DB tables for teams, players, contracts, picks; provide schema and indexes.
DevOps: containerization, CI/CD, test data seeding, and staging/prod environments.
Caching strategy for heavy reads (e.g., rosters, standings). Choose Redis vs. DB caching.
Auth/session model for single-player now and future multi-user leagues.
Telemetry/metrics plan: what to log and how to analyze balancing issues.
Front-end build plan: Next.js + Tailwind + shadcn; state management and routing.
Performance targets and profiling plan (CPU/memory) for season sims at scale.
